reviews.review_description: I cant find a game says to update but it doesn't have one rn i reinstalled it and everything i wish the game wasnt dieing

20260307:  update broke it.  absolutely does not load. 

20260222:

i got this game bc synth riders is pretty awesome and the support and community there are great, and fighting games are more up my alley anyway (i have all the majors, and many minor titles also).

unfortunately, the controls here are extremely confusing and unintuitive - they need better tutorials at the least, and imo perhaps a rewrite or completely different options (eg why have to hold b to strafe when left and right are available anyway??). no other fighting games move in such a strange and unnatural way. 

game play seems unintuitive also, eg i can't tell why another player hit me when i thought i hit him at the same time or even earlier.  maybe that's more like street fighter than thrill of the fight, so I'm just not used to it.  sometimes i go out of body (like for a throw) and then I'm not facing my opponent, and since I'm out of body i can't adjust to face him to block.  perhaps that's intentional, but it's very annoying watching that for several seconds helplessly as the guy pummels me.  i don't recall this kind of thing happening in street fighter.

punching seems to be rarely used in favor of supers, and supers sometimes don't fire even though i believe I'm doing the moves exactly right and exactly like i always do.

there are also glitches. i just played in the tournament and i crushed one guy first round, but then it totally froze.  i waited over 10 minutes so i wouldn't lose my win, and finally gave up and quit, hoping my points would be waiting for me.  they weren't.  i don't know if the guy legit lost connection, or cheesed me by quitting after i put the beat down.  anyway, not a great experience. 

i see potential, but for now there's a lot of room for improvement.

reviews.review_title: I LIKED GAME, GAME HATES ME
reviews.review_description:
This game was so much fun to learn. I was really into learning and thinking I was going to be good 

It royally kicks my butt. It was so hard that I not only wanted to just break my headset, which is not a thing I ever ever ever feel, but I thoroughly did not want to play the game anymore. 

That's my experience. 
And I'm 43 with a background in everything from Street fighter to mortal Kombat, pretty hardcore... So yeah. 
That's probably why I left the meanest review I've ever left before this one.

But I'll give it two stars because it's probably a great game, but why would anybody make it so hard beats me. 
(I really tried)

Absolute worst game that I’ve played on the vr to date, no wonder this is free with horizon+, none of the mechanics work correctly and the game has a utter lack of content to begin with. Edit** tried play this again only for it to be worse, do not buy

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for taking the time to leave a review. We’re genuinely sorry to hear the experience didn’t land for you.

FINAL FURY is still in Early Access, even while being available on Meta Horizon+, and we know that means some mechanics and systems are still evolving. We can understand the frustration if you were expecting a fully complete product - we're on the path but not there yet! However, if certain things felt utterly broken or frustrating on your end, we absolutely want to understand what wasn’t working for you so we can improve it.

If you’re open to it, we’d really appreciate more detail on what mechanics felt off or what content felt lacking. The fastest way to reach us and have your feedback seen by the dev team is via our Discord, where we actively track issues and suggestions from players.

We also keep a public roadmap on our official website that outlines what’s currently in development and what’s coming next. There’s a lot more content and polish on the way, and player feedback plays a big part in shaping that.

Thanks again for giving it a shot, even if it wasn’t for you right now. We'd appreciate it if you returned in a few months to see if the game has improved for you at that time.

reviews.review_title: Final is in the name for a reason
reviews.review_description: Why is the game so boring because It had a great premise for bringing a tournament fighter to VR but I just fall flat at everything

developer_response.body:
When you say it fell short of the premise, we’d genuinely love to understand where the disconnect was for you. Was it the pacing, the mechanics, the variety, or how the tournament fighter idea translates into VR moment to moment? Different players come in expecting very different things, and that gap is exactly what we’re trying to close.

FINAL FURY is in Early Access and very much not a finished product. We’re actively developing it alongside the community, adjusting systems, depth, onboarding, and overall feel based on real player feedback, not guesses. Our public roadmap lays out what’s coming and what we’re actively working toward here:
https://finalfuryvr.com/final-fury-roadmap/

If you’re up for it, we’d really like to make this game land for you. Jump into our Discord and tell us what didn’t hit or what you hoped it would be. That input directly shapes where the game goes next.
https://discord.gg/finalfury

reviews.review_description: If you're buying this for the multiplayer don't it'll connect you to the person you're playing with but would never let you in a match together you'll be stuck on a black screen waiting for a game that won't let you play

developer_response.body:
Hi there!

Sorry to hear you had issues connecting to multiplayer. 

What we believe happened here was that you were caught between the old and new versions of the game.

Could you force an update or reinstall please, that should fix the problem.

If you're still having issues please contact us via our official website (finalfuryvr.com) or Discord (https://discord.gg/finalfury) and we'll help troubleshoot this further.

Thanks!

reviews.review_description: the new update is garbage, full of bugs and the AI Input read and abuses cheap moves . Cant give zero stars on it but i can say its one of the worst fighting games in vr.

developer_response.body:
Hey there, really sorry to hear the update landed badly for you. That’s never the experience we want players to have, and we totally get how frustrating bugs and AI behaviour can be when you’re just trying to have fun.

FINAL FURY is still in Early Access, so a lot of systems are actively evolving as we move toward full launch. We’re committed to improving the experience with our players, not in a vacuum, and feedback like yours genuinely helps us prioritise what needs attention first.

You didn’t mention specific bugs or which AI moves felt cheap, but we’d really love to dig into that with you so we can fix it. If you’re up for it, sharing a little more detail would help us massively and get those issues sorted faster. Give us a message on our Discord (discord.gg/finalfury) or official site if you get a moment please!

Either way, thanks for giving it a shot, and we hope we can win you back as the game keeps improving

reviews.review_description: cant even play it only a dark screen

developer_response.body:
Hey there,

Sorry to hear you're having trouble launching the game, there's several reasons that could be causing this, and the good news is that most of them are fixable.

This isn't the place to help you troubleshoot but we'd certainly like to fix this with you. Could you contact us via our official website, or via Discord (https://discord.gg/finalfury) please?

The quick thing I would suggest is that make sure you're on the correct version of the game - if you're on a different release channel it may not launch. 

Thanks!

reviews.review_description:
Need more levels need more characters Love the game you need to add to jump mechanic back More levels more and it need a graphic upgrade for the quest 3 
Your characters need more special moves in combos Heck if you can act of act like a fake tally kind of mood like a finishing move when you going to win you do a quick move and it does it how about adding some melee weapons in there where you can pick up Millie weapons and fight and use them how about add some crowd in the background to get a game of more kick any upgrades like upgrade your armor get better moves. Like you can add uppercut combos or you can punch them and then swing back and forth to punching while he in the air you can like completely punch in it or do uppercuts and knock them in the air are you swing left and right in the uppercut like combos where you do a combo where I do like a special mood where it goes into a scene where you tearing them all up and pieces and then you know you do the finishing move
I had lots of playing it though, the controls are kinda weird but once you get used to them it gets really fun

The immersive VR combat had me fully engaged. Each fighter's unique move set kept the battles fresh, and I loved the challenge of adapting my strategy to outsmart my opponent. That moment of triumph, feeling like a true fighter in the ring, encapsulated the essence of Final Fury and made me eager to jump back in for more action! What made the experience even more enjoyable was playing in multiplayer with friends. Competing against each other added an extra layer of excitement and camaraderie, as we competed to take each other down. Additionally, Final Fury truly exercises my mind, pushing me to make quick decisions in the heat of battle. The fast-paced nature of the game requires constant awareness of my opponent's moves while planning my own attacks. It’s not just about physical prowess; the game challenges my reflexes and strategic thinking, making each match a thrilling blend of mind and body.

reviews.review_title: Bring Street Fighter in VR? Done ✅
reviews.review_description: Final Fury is an fun engaging VR fighting game with a concept that’s both easy to grasp and exciting to master. The combat mechanics feel natural and immersive, letting you focus on the thrill of each duel rather than fumbling with controls. It genuinely brought back the same rush I had playing classics like Street Fighter, but with a fresh VR twist. Punch, block, dodge — and feel like you're inside the fight. A must-play for fans of action and competitive games!

reviews.review_title: I don't think this game is salvageable
reviews.review_description:
I used to love playing games like Street Fighter, Tekken, and Mortal Kombat, but this attempt to adapt a fighting game for VR is a failure.

I'll give the game credit for its low price, which means you can check it out without burning too much money, and for the fact that the sound effects, especially the announcer, are well done. But that's about it for the positives.

Quite apart from the fact that the gameplay doesn't work particularly well in VR, the fighters have a very unappealing design, as do the arenas, and the technical presentation is also off-putting.

The resolution is the meager standard resolution of the Quest 3, and at 72 fps. With something like the Quest Game Optimizer, you can see that much more is possible in terms of resolution while still maintaining the framerate. For some reason, however, the developer decided against increasing the render target, serving the player a blurry image that doesn't have to be so muddy. And it's not just the resolution that's underwhelming; large parts of the rest of the presentation, including the effects, are as well. [It's clear that mobile SoCs don't have that much power due to limited TDP, etc., BUT they aren't so underpowered that this should be the result.] It looks a bit like it was made for the Quest 1.

Traditionally, fighting games on consoles have often been real eye-candy, visually pushing the limits of what was possible in their console generations. Here in VR, it's the exact opposite (an upscaled Tekken 4 from the PS2 era 24 years ago looks more sophisticated).

Sorry, but I don't think this game is salvageable. The developer, who previously made Synth Riders (which isn't a technical marvel either, but is at least fun due to its gameplay concept), should just move on to a different genre in VR.

Street Fighter X Smash X Tekken

This is a great fighting game built for VR with some traits of each of the fighting games I show above. 

I love the mechanics, and the game runs really well, even with using Quest Games Optimizer to bump the graphics. No stutters or lag 
to be found.

The presentation is great, and it’s really fun and challenging, especially with the final fight with the unlock-able hidden character. 

Hopefully the devs are cooking.. 

I would like to see these things added. 

More characters, more moves for each character, the ability to kick even if it’s a button press, a story mode, more arenas, and a more deep combo system through punching and kicking so the whole game doesn’t typically boil down to spamming projectiles, traps, or throwing. 

Still, for $10 it’s worth the cost especially if you have friends who own it that are into the genre.

I really enjoyed this game! It’s as close to an actual fighter as you can get in VR. It plays like ARMS, where your punches send your hands flying at your opponent which works even better in VR. You can do grabs and blocks, and each character has their own moves for you to perform. It’s one of those games based around reversals, trying to stun and wear down your opponent to get some good hits or grabs in. The stages are pretty good looking (Especially the area where you fight the last opponent in the single player) and the game can definitely get hectic and fun with a friend. This game is one that I definitely think anyone who likes fighting games and doesn’t mind swinging their arms a lot would enjoy this (Mines got a little sore from playing too long). I hope this game continues to get updates and new characters in the future, Recommend!
This game is such a disappointment. Was really hoping to be something like street fighter and it seems to be trying to do so but fails miserably. Can't even refund anymore, such a waste.

reviews.review_title: One for the Fighting Game Community?
reviews.review_description:
Final Fury is a pretty interesting step into the competitive VR fighting game genre

To me its almost a bit of a hybrid of the punch out series crossed with something like street fighter.

Every character has access to punches, a throw, a block, a dash and reversal.
Whilst also having access to 2 unique specials at launch.

The game is largely about the "footsies" game knowing the spacing of your attacks in between the openings. Spamming punches will just get you caught out by a defensive player who can land counter hits in between your punches (stopping you from attacking long enough to put them at an advantage)

Reversals are also useful to punch people out of punch spamming when needed.
Whilst throwing can counter defensive heavy players who block too much as the can only be dodged or "tech thrown" (e.g. timing your throw to clash with theirs)

Specials are there to add another layer of spice that shakes up the gameplay with their own unique properties.
Glitch's mines can funnel enemies approach, Tempests arctic blasts can be used for spacing/long distance attacks etc

Each of the 6 playable EA characters have their own strengths and weakness to play to.

Whilst it doesn't sound like many on paper, you have to remember that the games is still in EA.
This game is from the same team that brought us Synth riders.....a game that has only gone from strength to strength from its early applab days to the behemoth it is today in the VR music game genre.

Can the team strike it lucky with the VR 1v1 fighting game genre? Only time will tell its off to a promising start.
But with the legend that is Justin Wong! on board; behind the scenes.....the game has all the potential to be very special.

Its mixed recommendation at launch (Ill update the review with updates)......depending on if your the type that want single player content or one that likes to just take the fights online.

It reminds me a lot of Street Fighter 5 at launch with minimal stuff for solo players to dive into (just quick matches and a series of fights in the Arcade mode, complete with final boss)
But so much of the fun actually comes onto play in the online VS where real people come up with tactics that the AI can only dream off.

But as the game continues to expand with the addition of more characters, with the fine tuning of mechanics and character balance.
The game has so much potential into kickstarting the 1v1 fighter genre in VR.

Im here to see that journey happen!
More casual players may like to wait till the team adds in some more single player content (e.g. a story mode?)

reviews.review_title: Arcade combat in VR - let me at them!
reviews.review_description: This game puts you right in the front of the action and smacks you with it. The gestures are pretty cool even if initially hard to memorize. It helps with the suspension of disbelief really, as you move your hands about and use the gestures, moves just explode out towards your energy making the whole thing an absolute delight. Though some movements may require that you keep your arms in front of you because the cameras don’t pick the arms quite well when they are hidden in the back. Overall the game is solid. Hope the devs continue working on it and add more game modes

reviews.review_title: Competitive VR fighting at its best
reviews.review_description:
I've been a fan of 1v1 fighting games since my arcade days playing street fighter 2. Final Fury is trying to capture that magic in VR and so far it has done a great job.  You can throw, block, sidestep and do different special moves. I've seen in real time how these can be mixed up to form different strategies. Characters are unique from each other and not carbon copies. 
     As I stated before this is definitely a step in the right direction. I hope they continue to improve and balance the game as well as add new characters. It would be awesome to have a competitive VR scene and this game if off to a great start. I would recommend this to any fighting game fan.

This game gets constant attention from the developers, there are tournaments, and straight to the point: the game is REALLY good.  If you ever touched a fighting game, you owe it to yourself to get this game.

Super fun arcade type fighting experience - each character has their own special ability. Each one is fun to play in their own way. It’s fun but also can be super competitive when you fight people online. Would love some sort of ranked tournament mode or something.

All in all great fun, high fps, decent graphics - recommend.

reviews.review_title: Big step forward for VR fighting games!
reviews.review_description:
As a fan of classic fighters like Street Fighter and Tekken, I was blown away by how immersive and physical the combat feels in VR. Pulling off combos with actual punches and movement is incredibly satisfying. The character designs are great, the stages look slick, and the netcode works well for multiplayer.

It’s an early access, but the foundation is solid and super fun. Can’t wait to see how it evolves—highly recommend if you like action and own a VR headset.

I was hoping for a sporty boxing excercise game but it’s more a button presser even though it claims that it isn’t… the first character you play against just shoots at you over and over and over again which seems a bit unfair and then you need to get close to box, but then when they block you they explode… so you can’t fight close, you can’t find far… swing your arms ever so often and it does a little blob… so then you decide to play the character with the big guns and just shoot over and over again you don’t even need to use your arms. Played about 10 games lost them all, it’s mostly clicking to dash and punching forward and shooting… meh

Final Fury is an amazing fighting game that I had a lot of fun playing. Throughout every game mode, I had so much fun. I highly recommend it to people who are looking for a new fighting game. It was a nice workout too. I have never really liked active VR games but Final Fury is a definite exception. I will always come back to this game when I'm bored.

A great fighting game that is easy to learn but has a lot of depth and takes awhile to master.  While every fighter has common moves like punch and block they also each have two unique moves.  So you can easily get the moves down but needs a lot of practice to learn the best fighting styles for the different characters.  It gives the game a ton of variety in how you go about winning your fights.  Have an opponent who is mashing buttons sending flurries of punches your way, or someone who is constantly on the move will mean you react to them differently.  It really rewards you for learning how best to use both your basic and special moves.  One style might crush your opponent handily while another will lead to a loss.  All the characters are wildly different in appearance, I found them all both fun to play and face off against in a match.

reviews.review_title: A satisfying, crunchy new fighting game!
reviews.review_description: This game, while early in it's days, have managed to encapsulate the satisfaction of truly staring your opponent in the eye, and hitting them with everything you've got! I'm absolutely hooked at the satisfying look of eating attacks and finding the perfect windows for openings. Looking to outsmart my opponent on equal grounds and walking away with a well earned victory rarely feels exciting to me in flat screen anymore after really going head to head in Final Fury!

Was you childhood dream to be ported into a mortal combat or street fighter like game, if so your wish is about to be realized.  This is a 1 v 1 combat game where you are one of the many characters that you choose, all have special combat and defense abilities, you can play solo against the ai bots, or multiplayer against a friend or friend to be. Just make sure to put on your workout clothes this game is a true fun workout.

reviews.review_title: 2D to 3D fighters, a bit to get used to.
reviews.review_description:
Being someone who gree up with games like Mortal Kombat, Street fighter, and Tekken, I was thrilled to try this out. I was excited to see what I could unleash. 
First jumping in I was excited to see what I could accomplish in the game, and in the beginning I had no issues. But then came one thing I didnt forsee. Nausea. 
I play a lot of high intensity games, but the camera zooming in and out and changing angles was something I somehow couldnt stomach, and I couldnt find a setting to disable the dynamic camera from the time I played. 
Other than a slight bit of motion sickness, which those who may be prone to I advise getting used to vr motion first, I love the game and its dynamics. Its all balanced and very fun to play, and it reminds me a lot of Mortal Kombat: Deception and Armageddon. Definitely recommend to those who love games like fighters.

If you are looking for a brawler to fight the AI, or even better, your friends, this game might be the right one for you. The price is low too. So get it for yourself and gift it to a friend, and enjoy the action. This can be pretty intense to play. So keep that in mind, if fast movements of your arms are painful or when your movement is limited.

reviews.review_title: Has a chance to be one of the Greats!
reviews.review_description: Final Fury has consistently improved itself ever since I started playing this game a few months ago! I've also taken part in a few tournaments, safe to say that there're so many different play styles and also so many wonderful characters! The devs immediately take feedback and criticism and work on them! Great game with great skills and combat, can't recommend more!!

This game is super fun, nostalgic, and a decent workout! It definitely pays homage to early flatscreen fighter games, but lets you use your whole body and really get into. 

I wouldn’t classify this as a boxing game, so if that’s what you’re looking for, other games will suit you better. This in no way approaches the experience of a realistic boxing workout. But if you’re looking for that old-school charm from flatscreen combat games in a full-body experience , this is a ton of fun!

The game is in early access, so I took one star off for limited content, but what’s there is a blast. It also really shines in multiplayer mode.

reviews.review_title: Old school flair new school graphics
reviews.review_description: The fighting game you didn't know you needed, for me i was working up a sweat while i got my fight on in the game and thats a win win for me a good 2 for 1 if you ask me . final fury reminds me of the old school games we grew up with but with updated graphics for the new school gaming system. Here are some reasons why i like this game the fact that you can choose your character and master their abilities , you can choose single or multiplayer you can play against your friends or random players the choice is yours . everything about this game is great from the enemies to the graphics to the game play .

I really like the game, and its characters. But it’s SUPER Buggy. I was fighting a Hard CPU, and it wouldn’t stop using “Arctic Blast!” Another bug is with the Secret, unlockable character. When you change his skin, the flame on its head doesn’t change. And with the same character, it’s “Trail” becomes blue when the characters “clash” and not Red. And also, the CPUs keep spamming the same mover over, and over again. It took me 20 attempts to beat 1 CPU, because she spammed the same move. And when I was stunned, she spammed it even more. Overhaul, this game has really good characters, but is SUPER buggy.

Interfacing with the game is a little rigid but the overall mechanics are quite good. Simple to understand, but there's an obvious learning curve to really master it. Good work-out as well. 

Devs were quick to rectify an issue with playing this using Quest Pro controllers. If you're into fighting games and want to try one in VR, I doubt you'll find better than this.

This is really great and I enjoyed playing campaign and fighting all the different characters although there are certain characters that are very easy to spam with and are annoying to fight against there is usually a way to avoid the attacks by dodging. Also there is multiplayer mode where you can do online matches or make a code and fight a friend if you don’t feel like doing a public. Overall one of my favorite vr fighting games that you can get because it’s simple, fun, and makes you be strategic to win a fight.

Final Fury is a VR 1v1 fighting game that blends physical movement with classic fighting mechanics. 

You can punch, block, throw, dash, and use two unique special moves per character. The combat system rewards timing and positioning over button-mashing, with a focus on reading your opponent, executing reversals, and mixing up attacks. Each of the six (at the time of the review) characters has distinct abilities, strengths, and weaknesses, encouraging diverse playstyles.

The game offers both single-player and multiplayer modes. In single-player, you can go through arcade-style matches ending in a final boss battle. While the AI is decent at the start, it becomes predictable. Making a stronger AI system is a needed improvement, in my opinion. Multiplayer is where Final Fury truly shines—competing against real players online or with friends brings out the game’s strategic depth and chaotic fun.

Visually, the game features well-designed arenas, and gameplay mechanics feel responsive. It also includes core fighting game elements like counters, tech throws, and spacing control.



It's a great start with solid mechanics, but I’d love to see a story mode and better AI in future updates. Still, it’s a fun experience that’s worth checking out, especially if you're into VR and fighting games.

Final Fury brings an exciting, arcade-style VR fighting experience with smooth mechanics and well-balanced AI opponents. Multiplayer shines, making each match against friends a fun test of skill. The roster offers variety, though some character moves - like Niko’s - can feel difficult to trigger.

While the combat is enjoyable, I personally would have liked a few more basic attack options, such as uppercuts, to add variety to the move sets. Some of the characters’ special moves feel similar, and adding more distinction between them could make fights even more dynamic. It would also be fun to see destructible elements in the arenas - like columns breaking on impact - bringing an extra layer of immersion.

Despite these personal preferences, Final Fury is a solid fighter that’s easy to pick up and keeps players coming back - especially for multiplayer battles and new character releases. Definitely worth checking out!

This is the best 1v1 fighting game I’ve tried in vr.  Reminds me of days playing street fighter.
     There are six unique fighter to pick from during the ea.  Every fighter is artistically different not just some cookie cutter model. Each fighter has 2 special moves as well.  
The combat is a combination of punches throws blocks and counters.  You can also dodge by sidestepping around.  The game is balanced so as with proper timing you can fight with plenty of styles.  
    The gameplay keeps the fights interesting enough with every fighter having it own special to keep things interesting.   
    I have high hopes for this game. I could see potential for a great competitive community.

reviews.review_title: First really good VR arcade fighter?
reviews.review_description: Yeah I would say so. We've had fighting games before but this is a fast paced arcade style fighter like Tekken but played in VR. Great soundtrack, graphics, fun characters. What a great start so far! I had so much hope for this game and its lived up to that. Highly recommended!

reviews.review_title: This is the reason you bought VR
reviews.review_description:
When you were a kid and you used to play fighting games on the TV or at the arcade, did you ever imagine yourself being the character in real life? Did you ever pretend fight and shoot a hadouken and imagine yourself charging up a move to unleash it at the palm of your hands? This game is that. I could probably do a mic drop right now. That's all this review needs, but I believe in this game so much that I will continue. 

Think back to the first time you purchased a VR headset. You wanted an experience that you could not get playing on your TV, correct? Maybe you had one genre in mind, or maybe you had all genres in mind. To me, VR games that have changed or evolved the medium are games like Sairento, Hellsweeper, VR Chat, Resident Evil Village, Gran Turismo, and Witchblood. Those are my favorite VR games of all time. This one is now up there because it is the first of its kind.

Think of the first time you played a VR horror game, a VR racing game, a VR social game, think to the first time you played any genre in VR. This is that but for fighting games. 

The controls are perfect and the fighters are varied. Imagine if the game succeeds if they add more fighters. Every new fighting game has a small roster in its first iteration and the game just barely came out. I can not wait to see what else they improve on. And for ten dollars? This game isn't trying to rip you off. It's trying to thrive and succeed off of a bare minimum cost for the players because it's confident enough to believe in itself. 

They also got input from a fighting game world champion and it shows. Controls are tight, I like the characters, there are skins to unlock, there's multiplayer. 

I remember when I first used a Gear VR headset in 2016 that I played some fighting game on it where you used a normal controller and pressed buttons to fight like you would in any other fighting game. It didn't hold my attention for long but I appreciated that they released a fighting game at all and tried to do something different. And I thought to myself "One day, there will be a true VR fighting game. A REAL one." 

This is it. We finally have that VR fighting game. 

Final Fury.

I've always wanted a fantasy fighting game in VR. Final fury definitely scratches that itch. 

Implementing executable super moves that are also enjoyable definitely isn't an easy task but final fury perfected it. 

Love the ladders mode. Having unlockables is also fun. 

If you ever wondered how super moves will ever work in VR, this is the game to try and answer that.

reviews.review_title: Final Fury is an absolute banger!!!
reviews.review_description: A stunning blend of arcade-style fighting and full-body VR immersion—fast, flashy, and insanely fun! Every punch feels powerful, every move looks epic. If you’ve got a headset, this is the fight to step into.

It's a great, action-packed VR fighting game! You can choose from multiple fighters, each with unique abilities, and there's an arcade mode to unlock the final boss. Finish arcade mode with a character, and you get a reskin color that still looks original. This game is so addicting it makes you want to learn and master every character, And the people who made the game are very keen to listen to player feedback and want to make the game more balanced for the future of final fury.

reviews.review_title: Fun game with room for growth
reviews.review_description:
I really enjoy this game. Really no other games like it. It's street fighter in VR.  Characters are unique and each offer different abilities. The arenas all have their own style which is great. The combat feels smooth and works well in VR. Only problem is I've been having trouble finding matches online. The game is relatively new so hopefully down the line more people will be active. All in all I'm very satisfied with my purchase.

PS: Just my 2 cents. Down the line I think adding a few more characters would be a nice add on. Also, maybe add objects in certain arenas that can damage your opponent. That would also spice things up a bit. The great thing about this game is you can really run wild with ideas. I look forward to seeing the progress.

1st impressions: Very promising, so far. No, it ain’t Soul Caliber, yet. But the mechanics are there, and this VERY successfully transitions the spirit of flat screen fighting to VR. A huge accomplishment. Kudos to the Devs. Well worth the price 👍👍

This game combines the fun of boxing with interesting powers. I'd describe it as something akin to Arms on the switch. The characters have varied types of designs and attacks and it's an easy to learn but hard to master kind of game. Looking forward to seeing how this game evolved as more content is added. Highly recommend.
Disclaimer; I received this game free of charge for playtesting

reviews.review_title: One of a kind VR fighting
reviews.review_description: Very reminisent of games like tekken but in first person ans VR. Cool characters all with unique abilities, animations, and voice lines. It has a decent tutorial and the solo ladder is fun to yo through. There are a variety of maps keeping everything feeling fresh each time you play. Overall if you are a fan of flat screen fighting games like tekken, mortal kombat, or street fighter you should give this game a try!

Final Fury is a fighting game with the benefit of making you literally sweat for your wins. No button mashing, move your arms to perform your attacks. For that, the move sets of each character is kept simple enough to not constantly perform the wrong attack or forget which different arm movements do which attacka. Characters are also kept distinct in both playstyle and design, theyre quick to learn and are not overwhelming. The only complaint i would have is the seemingly unbalanced nature of having characters with vastly different ranges, especially gaia seems really strong to me, but that could also be me not being good in fighting games in general, so don't take my opinion too strongly.
If you see me ingame, go easy on me please :)

Great to see the creators of Synth Riders branching out from a rhythm to fighting game. The game is polished, easy to pickup, and hard to master. You start with 5 fighters to choose from with 2 more to unlock. The fighters available have different skill sets that range from close in fighting to a mix to far ranged. My fav is Glitch because he has a ranged style. There are multiple ways to play plus single and multi-player modes. Lots of fun sobe found here.

When i played final fury i felt i was forcing myself to play it instead of actually wanting to come back and play it. I think the reason for this is because it became very repetitive and was all in all not enough content. The reason i gave 2 stars was because i still think this game has great potential but as for now i did not enjoy it.

Final Fury is mostly like playing Street Fighter in VR. It is early access, so there are only 6 characters, but I'm sure they will add more. Everyone has 2 specials and they feel unique, but I think there needs to be more attacks. The game looks good and runs well. There is single player Arcade, and multiplayer.

If side scrolling turned into 1st person fighting game, this is it. The movement is very restricted with forward, back and dashes (sides/forward/back), and no jumping. I think its purpose is to make player play tactically. The best part of the game is motion gesture for skills but it still has some rough edges to polish. Some characters are so easy to use you can spam projectile just by lifting and slaming down your hands definitely some balancing is needed. I hope future update will. Make the gameplay more exciting.

This game is really good. It feels like Arms meets street fighter and super smash bros. The characters are good and well made, the combat is fun, and the bots actually feel like a challenge(you have no idea how bad fighting game bots can be). It feels really well put together and you can tell the devs cared about this game. It’s literally one of a kind vr game that you will not regret playing. I hope they continue to update it and make it better than it already is.

This game has alot of different ways to play and has a fighter for pretty much everyone.

The game doesn't go shy on the maps either!

The visuals are done very well and in the end of the day, you ACTUALLY feel like you are in a fighting game.

Overall, I give this game a 4/5 stars.

-⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️-

reviews.review_title: An enjoyable step forward in VR fighters
reviews.review_description:
Kluge have made an intriguing pivot after the success of Synth Riders nobody would have seen coming and created an interesting and fun entry into the VR fighting game scene.

Use your hands to beat your friends into submission, use your arms and some great gestures to cast a projectile and feel closer to actually being able to throw a fireball at your opponent or circle around to close the distance and throw them into the ground. 

The gameplay is fast and frantic yet can also be slowed down with a little practise to make the fights more tactical and precise. Online play can devolve into frantic spam fests but the characters have their own unique move set to counter such matches with a little practice.

The only reason for a 4 over a 5 rating is due to the currently smaller and limited roster than many may expect from a fighting game. However as balancing a fighting game  is difficult normally, let alone in vr so I appreciate them taking some time between fighter releases.

After how the developers have shown the lengths they go to when supporting their game post-launch, I keenly await what they come up with next to open this game up for repeated pummelling of friends and strangers when I want a little of that virtual Street Fighter feel that isn't currently available anywhere else. Well worth a try if you're interested in trying a VR Fighter out.

reviews.review_title: I felt like a super hero
reviews.review_description: This was a blast to play, make sure you play all the characters and find your style. I love the gesture moves, it really adds to the gameplay.   I hope that they come out with more characters to play. I look forward to seeing what other gesture moves they come up with. A warning, start with small sessions, I got too carried away with playing that the next day was painful, but worth it. Great game, great jobs Devs.

reviews.review_title: A Must-Try for VR Fighting Fans
reviews.review_description:
Final Fury brings old-school 1v1 combat to VR with style and panache. It rewards clever thinking just as much as fast reactions. Each character has a unique move set with blocks, throws, specials, and reversals, keeping every match competitive and fresh.

Multiplayer is where the game truly comes alive — battling friends brings out the mind games, clutch reversals, and adrenaline-fueled moments. Just beware of that one friend who thinks spamming punches is a viable strategy... until they get parried into next week.

With six characters at launch Final Fury is off to a fantastic start. It's a fast, fun workout and a great leap forward for VR fighting games.

Highly, highly recommended for competitive VR players and fight fans.

I really liked Final Fury. It’s kind of like other fighting games, but I feel like they use the device it’s played on really well. It feels like Street Fighter, but in VR — and I really like that. It’s not exactly my type of game, but it’s still really enjoyable.

I have been interested in this title ever since it was announced. I am a fan of Synth Riders (the developer's previous VR title) and fighting games, so I couldn't wait to give it a try.

My impression is very positive considering this is Early Access. 

My main positives are:
+ Varied characters in terms of playstyle
+ I like the character art design
+ The motions required to do special moves are easy to remember and fit well with the attacks
+ Matches against the AI can provide a solid challenge
+ Multiplayer matches can be really fun.

My negatives are:
- I would like to see more fighters made available - mainly because the other characters are already very good and I want more. I expect more to be added
- "attack spam" can be annoying to play against. 
- there are some times when the attack that I intended to do isn't the one that is performed.

Overall, there is a lot to like already. If you were interested from the trailers or from other previews/reviews, I definitely recommend giving it a try.

reviews.review_title: Like a mix of SSB and Street fighter
reviews.review_description:
The first thing that I think of when I play this game is that it’s kinda like Super Smash Bros but in first person. The selection of characters is enough to keep you playing for hours trying to master combo’s. The graphics were amazing and reminded me of old school street fighter. 

The controls were easy to learn and I liked using gestures for the controls although it was buggy at times. Sometimes the controls would get mixed up for some reason and other times, they would t register at all.

The characters designs were amazing and looked as if they came out of an animation or drawing.

Overall, I loved the game and will be playing again. 9.5/10

I would give this less stars, but I don't want it to bomb, I understand it's in early access and tbh I got hyped when Justin Wong appeared with their seal of approval.

I have played plenty of regular fighting games for a while, and also VR fighting games.

I understand it's difficult to define a new genre on a platform like this, however I feel like this is far from done.

I really want to return the game, and I have the timeframe to do it, however I know Kluge will deliver. And I got this at a very fair price.

I’m honestly surprised I like this game at first I was like eh until I really started to get into
It all the characters felt fun and unique and fighting against friends was amazing and Dosent get boring the graphics are what you would expect from a quest game which is honest ok as gameplay is what really matters but I had a ton a fun and I’m sure you will to

Really loved this game. Street fighter with a mix of Synth Riders. Really felt the SR influence as I was bobbing and weaving and smashing opponents. Great workout. The developers have a way of drawing you into whatever genre they tackle and they delivered a very immersion experience with a great visual style. Would recommed.

Single player is too easy. I never lost a game against bots. Much more fun and challenging in multiplayer mode.
 I took single mode to know the characters and their powers. 

It would be great, if there was also a leaderboard, with the win%. That would make it more challenging. 

The graphics are very good.

After playing about 5 matches, I already was out of breath. So it is a good workout.

This is a great idea for VR. Doing gestures for fighting game moves is fun. There is a good variety in the different character movesets. The game has a ton of potential.

However, it feels like a demo because there is almost no content. The only thing you can do is simple fights. The closest thing to a campaign (called "Ladder") is just fighting all the characters back to back. I am not interested in playing multiplayer, so maybe you will see that as content that I don't.

A  fun 1v1 fighting game similar to mortal combat with each character having their own fighting move set and styles there's a playstyle for everyone. With gesture based attacks get immersed in the combat with physical attacks instead of button mashing. The gestures were pretty intuitive to what each attack did and it didn't take long to start having fun fighting without really needed to think about the different moves.

FINAL FURY nails what VR fighting should feel like. The second I slipped on my headset, I stepped into the ring and instantly felt part of the action—no lazy button-mashing here. Throwing real punches, blocking instinctively, and blasting out special moves with my own arms made every round a workout (prepare to sweat!). The cast is varied; each fighter offers something different, so you can find your favorite playstyle.

Playing online was smooth; barely any lag killed the vibe. The Arcade Mode packs tough AI that keeps matches exciting and unlocks add solid replay value. While the roster sits at just six for now and I'd love more stage variety, what's here is polished and intense.

The soundtrack gets your adrenaline going, too! FINAL FURY captures that classic arcade punch-up energy—and makes you work for it.

reviews.review_title: A Polished but Lightweight VR Brawler
reviews.review_description:
If you’ve played Synth Riders, you’ll recognize the polish here. Final Fury is a 1-on-1 arcade fighter where each character has its own satisfying mix of punches, dashes, and combo gestures. The core action feels great—easy to pick up, tough to master.

Content is on the lighter side right now, but what’s there is fun and well-made. Don’t expect a deep story; do expect fast, goofy bouts you can enjoy with friends or solo while you sharpen your skills. If you’re after straightforward VR fighting fun, this is a solid grab.

reviews.review_title: Has a classic feel to it
reviews.review_description:
This has a classic 80’s arcade fighter style feel to it. It certainly feels inspired from things like Virtua Fighter and Street Fighter, but does it in its own way. It is a first person fighter, but not all up in your face like most boxing games. Your punches are kind of cast, as well as having various unique fight moves per character. There are only 5 fighters to start, you unlock one more by completing the arcade mode one time on any difficulty. Some of the fighters can literally just be spam fighters and you can easily win nearly every time. Others take a little more to cast their ability, so they are a little harder to spam specific moves but still fun in their own way. There are custom paints for each character and there are 3 that can be unlocked by winning the arcade mode with that character on each of the 3 difficulties. As far as any progression, that is it. The only other thing this game has for it is multiplayer and that can be hit or miss. They have set up some events to try and get more people in the fights, but it is just win/lose, do it again.

This game has a lot of potential if it had more reward for playing it other than just a color change. I love the style, I really enjoy the different characters, but it just doesn’t have much going for it after that. It feels like there could be a whole backstory type fight with each of the characters. Sadly, there was more content in all the EULA, TOS, Privacy policy, Health and safety warning, COC and welcome message that you had to scroll and accept at the start than there is in the rest of the game.

reviews.review_title: Great concept but needs some work
reviews.review_description:
This game caught my attention with its promise of bringing classic arcade fighting game mechanics into the immersive world of Vr. As a fan of titles like Tekken the concept of physically executing moves in a virtual arena was intriguing.

The game features a diverse roster of characters each with unique abilities and fighting styles. The visual design is vibrant and the arenas are well crafted providing an engaging backdrop for battles.

However while the idea is compelling the execution left me wanting more. The gesture based controls intended to make you feel like you're truly in the fight often felt unresponsive or awkward. Performing special moves required specific hand motions that didn't always register correctly disrupting the flow of combat. 

Additionally the combat mechanics seemed to lack depth. While each character has distinct moves the absence of more complex systems like combos or counters made the gameplay feel repetitive over time. Some players noted that spamming basic attacks could be more effective than attempting strategic play which diminishes the game's potential for competitive depth.

Despite these issues Final Fury is still in Early Access and the developers have shown a commitment to refining the experience. I will be coming back to this game in the future.

This is a unique game where you can 1v1 a person with different characters with their own abilities, which is really intriguing and awesome in the game.

You can battle against bots or you can challenge a friend to destroy him in the game.

reviews.review_title: Fun game, a bit rough around the edges
reviews.review_description:
As a desktop fighting game player, I was very excited to see this game. It's fun and maintains a lot of the fundamental decisionmaking, but it definitely feels distinct from the genre. It feels most similar to ARMS. While it's fun, it does still have the main problem plaguing fighting games: it's hard to learn enough to play with intentionality, and it's easy to just end up mashing and get through most bot fights. There is more beneath that skill barrier, but it would be nice to see the game have better resources for learning such. 

DISCLAIMER: I received this game for free, for playtesting and review. The developer was given no input or oversight on my review, nor was I given expectation of rating the game well.

This is a great game for those who like fighting games or wished that fighting games would be available for VR, the gameplay is great and the tutorial is detailed enough for anyone to understand what's going on, overall n great game.

reviews.review_title: Polished, but too restrictive for VR
reviews.review_description:
Starting off, I was very pleased with the buttery smooth performance of Final Fury, considering the fact that the general game visuals are nice and at times better than current quest games. Though the menu is very boring, unmmersive and seems lacking work. It is as simple as a skybox and a straightforward flat laserpointer menu with some visual flare as well. It could be so much more! And that applies to the main gameplay of Final Fury too.
Final Fury might as well be a flatscreen game, which is sad to say, but that's the problem Final Fury has. There is no shortage of aspects like movement, menus, startup logo, cutscenes, animations, basic combat and input that are just straight up copied from flatscreen gaming without adjusting to the possibilities VR gives. Biggest Problem is the restrictiveness of your movement. Superhot did it great and let you dodge bullets by having you *move your body*, Final Fury only allows you to dodge by *pressing a button*, which moves you sideways. In my opinion this is the biggest backstep in VR fighting I've seen to date.
But with how much effort was put into designing the charachters, writing lore and polishing the visual style, I can say that Final Fury is only for those who don't mind seeing constant missed possibilities.

Everything feels intuitive in this game, from the way you punch to the way the combos are executed and the leveling. There isn't much in terms of unlocking things besides different color characters and maybe. 1-2 characters that I am unsure of how to unlock. It's more about the journey and the feeling of "leveling" different characters in it is what makes it fun as you have to fight and a decent level even with characters that you may not be used to. This has literally just dropped so I am not going to hate on it too much but it does have a bit less content than I would hope but this could easily change in the future. In order for a game like this to hold an audience for a long time I believe it needs more combos than 2 per character, this game would benefit from more complex multi move combos or many moves that can be chained into a combo of the users choice. But overall all the content we have works well feels smooth and is able to be executed fairly easily but there is still a reasonable skill gap and with more time which this title has it would deserve a five star. In my opinion this is the best or second best fighting game with similar aspects like this on quest standalone and I would suggest it to anyone looking for a VR fighter that is more than just boxing.

reviews.review_title: The best street fighter style game ever
reviews.review_description: Absolutely great I wish from more characters  beacause of oh good it was I might even grow a hobbie of this and maybe even a career game of the year for fighting games

This game never makes me bored to fight your friends and try new people o did I mention there is an arcade a mode where you fight all the people and if you win you get a new person and a skin for ho you wined with like I have every single skin on glitch

reviews.review_title: Old school fighter with a new beginning!
reviews.review_description: As a retro fan of 1 vs 1 combat fighters I was excited off the bat for this one but also sceptical. From the start there’s a little bit of a learning curve to get the hang of what and how you need to do things but eventually it just clicks. This is where the fun really starts. The game doesn’t take itself too seriously and remembering this pays dividends, the combat feels sharp and accurate, nothing is too predictable. Characters are nice and what you want from a game like this. As for the meat of the game it does what it needs too but there is plenty of room for growth and evolution, no doubt that will happen being the first of its kind. I don’t want to go too deep into this one as the best way to put it would be if your a fan of MK or SF series then this likely will be up you alley. To sum up I had plenty of fun with room for improvement.

reviews.review_title: Fighting Game from the arcades, now VR
reviews.review_description: Early Access version tested for free. This is a game in a Street Fighter style translated into VR. You can fight against others or the computer. There are different button and arm movement combinations that you have to memorize and execute to land hits or dodge/block, so a good translation of the arcade classics into VR, where YOU are the fighter. Can/will also be a workout if you want. While I enjoyed the gameplay, I think probably online matches vs others is the way to get longer term replayability if you like this style of game. Recommend to use QGO or other ways to make the graphics look better.

Very promising vr fighter with good engagement for a decent workout!!! Good combat design and move sets to give each character personality with an easy to understand tutorial to find your favorite character and playstyle.. For the price I would recommend giving it a shot!! Great start guys!!! Keep up the good work
